aliases: Pú táo yòu bào bào (yuán wèi)

romanized: Pú táo yòu bào bào (yuán wèi)

food_subgroup: Fruit tea with real fruit pulp and green tea base

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for grapefruit-green-tea fruit tea with real pulp. FOODON_03301710 (fruit-flavoured tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific code exists for grapefruit fruit tea with pulp; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 (Tea drink) is the closest parent. No specific code for grapefruit-green-tea fruit tea exists in SNOMED CT.

preparation_method: blended with real grapefruit slices and pulp

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one 400 ml cup, as typically served at HeyTea outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Grapefruit juice, raw (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 786619) and Green tea, brewed (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 173228) combined with cane sugar addition; proportional blend estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for HeyTea Grapefruit Boom. Nutrient values are estimated from a proportional blend of: (1) USDA FDC grapefruit juice raw (FDC 786619) weighted at ~50% of liquid volume representing grapefruit content, (2) USDA FDC brewed green tea (FDC 173228) weighted at ~40%, and (3) cane sugar contribution (~10% by weight, approximately 10 g sugar per 100 g beverage) based on typical bubble-tea sugar levels reported in peer-reviewed literature (Huang et al., 2021, Nutrients). The default preparation uses real cane sugar; values reflect the standard sweetened version. A no-sugar variant would have lower energy and sugar values.

gi_source.title: Grapefruit juice GI reference and sugar-sweetened fruit tea analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HeyTea Grapefruit Boom. GI estimated at 48 based on: (1) grapefruit juice GI of approximately 48 (low GI, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables), (2) green tea has negligible GI contribution, (3) added cane sugar (sucrose GI ~65) diluted by the dominant grapefruit-juice matrix. Weighted blend yields an estimated GI of ~48 (low). Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (48 × 40.8 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 19.6. Serving carbohydrate = 10.2 g per 100 g × 4 = 40.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Grapefruit–drug interaction and moderate sugar load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that grapefruit and grapefruit juice can inhibit CYP3A4 enzymes, potentially raising plasma levels of calcium channel blockers and other antihypertensive medications; patients on such medications should consult their physician before consuming this beverage regularly. Sodium content is low (~12 mg per 400 ml serving), which is favourable for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added cane sugar and glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: The standard sweetened version contains approximately 39 g of sugar per 400 ml serving (predominantly added cane sugar), resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 19.6, which is moderate-to-high for a single beverage; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request the no-sugar-added option and monitor postprandial blood glucose, as even the unsweetened version contains naturally occurring grapefruit sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grapefruit compounds (furanocoumarins) inhibit intestinal CYP3A4 and can significantly increase plasma concentrations of statins such as simvastatin and atorvastatin, raising the risk of myopathy; patients on statin therapy should avoid regular consumption of this beverage and discuss alternatives with their cardiologist. The beverage is low in saturated fat and cholesterol, which is otherwise favourable for cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grapefruit Boom (Original) is a signature fruit tea beverage from HeyTea, a Chinese tea brand with outlets in Singapore including Marina Bay Sands. The drink is made with real grapefruit slices and grapefruit pulp combined with a brewed green tea base, giving it a refreshing citrus flavour with a slightly bitter, tangy profile characteristic of grapefruit balanced by the mild astringency of green tea. The original version is sweetened with real cane sugar by default, though customers can request no sugar added or adjust the sweetness level. The beverage has been on the menu since 2016 and is considered a flagship product of the HeyTea brand. Per 100 g, the standard sweetened version provides approximately 42 kcal, 10.2 g carbohydrate (predominantly sugar at 9.8 g), 0.3 g protein, and 0.1 g fat, making it a low-fat, high-sugar beverage. It provides a meaningful amount of vitamin C (~15 mg per 100 g) from the grapefruit content. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low GI category) based on the grapefruit juice GI reference, but the glycemic load per standard 400 ml serving is approximately 19.6, which is moderate-to-high due to the volume of added sugar. Clinically, the most significant concern is the well-documented grapefruit–drug interaction: furanocoumarins in grapefruit inhibit intestinal CYP3A4, which can elevate plasma concentrations of statins, calcium channel blockers, and other medications. Patients on such medications should consult their physician before regular consumption. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the no-sugar-added option is strongly preferred. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is suitable for halal diets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in HeyTea Grapefruit Boom?
Does grapefruit tea interact with medications?
How much sugar is in Grapefruit Boom?
What is the GI of grapefruit tea?
Can I drink grapefruit tea on statins?
What is in HeyTea Grapefruit Boom Original?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for HeyTea Grapefruit Boom exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from a proportional blend of USDA FDC grapefruit juice raw and brewed green tea with cane sugar addition. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database grapefruit juice reference. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on direct laboratory analysis of this specific commercial product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values reflect the standard sweetened version (cane sugar default). A no-sugar-added variant would have approximately 20–22 kcal per 100 g and ~2.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. 2. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — grapefruit juice and green tea contain negligible saturated fat per USDA FDC; this is naturally zero for this beverage type. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero for this fruit-tea beverage. 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero for this fruit-tea beverage. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — no hydrogenated fats present. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-based beverage; B12 is not present in plant foods. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — no vitamin D source in this beverage. 9. glycemic_index set to 48 (low GI) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database grapefruit juice reference (Atkinson et al. 2021). 10. Grapefruit–drug interaction (CYP3A4 inhibition by furanocoumarins) is a clinically significant concern documented in peer-reviewed literature; this is flagged in all three health context entries. 11. The sugar-free sweetener option noted in the original description is currently out of stock per the field survey listing; default preparation uses cane sugar. 12. All -1 sentinel values: potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are populated with estimated values from USDA FDC blend; no -1 sentinel values remain in this record as all fields were estimable from the USDA FDC component sources.

Estimated nutrient values from USDA FDC grapefruit juice raw and brewed green tea blend with cane sugar addition, as no direct source exists.
Set glycemic_index to 48 (numeric >0) based on University of Sydney GI Database grapefruit juice reference; documented analogous estimate basis.

Documented all zero macro values (saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg) with naturally zero or confirmed zero justifications in notes.
